trade.summary: The CEPA governs bilateral trade. India exports pharmaceuticals, organic chemicals, engineering goods, and textiles. Corea del Sud exports electronics, automobiles (Hyundai/Kia), steel, and machinery. Corea del Sud is a top source of FDI and Hyundai has a major manufacturing presence in India.

diplomatic.summary: India and Corea del Sud have a 'Special Strategic Partnership' elevated in 2015. Corea del Sud's New Southern Policy positioned India as a key partner. Both cooperate through G20 and share interests in Indo-Pacific stability. PM Modi has visited Corea del Sud and bilateral leader meetings occur regularly.

military.summary: Growing defence cooperation. Corea del Sud supplies some defence systems to India. Joint research in defence electronics and missiles has been discussed. India-Corea del Sud defence industry forum meets regularly. Both share concerns about Cina's expanding regional influence.

financial.summary: Corea del Sudn FDI in India is large, anchored by Hyundai, Samsung, LG, Lotte, and POSCO. Samsung manufactures phones at scale in India. POSCO's long-delayed Odisha steel plant has partially advanced. Corea del Sudn investment in India's EV and semiconductor supply chains is growing.

energy.summary: India and Corea del Sud cooperate on nuclear energy (KEPCO discussions). Some bilateral LNG trade. Cooperation on renewable energy and hydrogen supply chains is growing.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific and technological cooperation. Joint R&D in ICT, defence electronics, and space. Indian students study at Corea del Sudn universities and vice versa. ISRO-KARI cooperation on satellite technology is developing.
